name: Bauhaus（包豪斯）
guidance: 大胆的几何现代主义，包含圆形、方形和三角形。主色调（红/蓝/黄），边缘鲜明，阴影强烈。功能性与艺术性兼备，带有建构主义的不对称。

name: 1. 极简主义品牌官网与电商

The Bauhaus style embodies the revolutionary principle "form follows function" while celebrating pure geometric beauty and primary color theory. On mobile, this translates to tactile constructivism—the screen is a canvas where elements are physically stacked blocks. The aesthetic creates a high-impact, poster-like experience in the palm of the hand: bold, touch-centric, and unapologetically graphic.
guidance: Vibe: Tactile, Constructivist, Geometric, High-Contrast, Bold, Architectural

name: Core Concept: The mobile interface is a vertical geometric composition. Scroll views are not just content streams but stacked layers of colored paper and rigid shapes. The limited screen real estate forces a focus on massive typography and distinct color blocking (Red #D02020, Blue #1040C0, Yellow #F0C020). Every tap should feel mechanical and substantial.
● Geometric Purity: UI elements are strictly circles (buttons/avatars) or squares (cards/inputs).
guidance: ● Tactile Depth: Hard shadows indicate interactivity; elements look "pressable."

name: ● Vertical Rhythm: Strong vertical stacking with deliberate spacing, avoiding clutter.
guidance: ● Constructivist Typography: Headlines are massive (text-4xl to text-5xl) relative to screen width, breaking traditional mobile scaling rules.

name: ● Thumb-Friendly: Interactive zones are large, distinct, and geometrically defined.

name: Core Principle
guidance: The Pocket Editorial. On mobile, Minimalist Monochrome transforms the screen into a tactile, high-end printed manifesto. It rejects the "app-like" tendency for soft bubbles and friendly gradients. Instead, it offers a stark, vertical journey defined by pure black (#000000), pure white (#FFFFFF), and razor-sharp borders. Every tap is a deliberate decision; every scroll is a flip of a page in a luxury monograph.

name: Visual Vibe
guidance: Emotional Keywords: Tactile, Austere, Editorial, Direct, High-Fidelity, Uncompromising, Sharp, Rhythmic.

1. Vertical Linearity
guidance: The mobile screen is a continuous roll of paper. Structure is created not by boxes, but by horizontal lines (rules) that span the full width of the device. Content lives between these lines.

name: 2. Typography as Interface
guidance: Buttons are often just large words. Navigation is text-based. The serif typeface (Playfair Display) acts as the primary image on the screen. Headlines must be large enough to break words onto new lines, creating graphic shapes.

name: 3. The "Touch" Inversion
guidance: Since there is no "hover" on mobile, interaction is communicated through inversion. When a user touches a white block, it turns black instantly. This zero-latency binary feedback replaces physical tactility.

name: 4. Zero Radius, Zero Fluff
guidance: All elements—buttons, images, inputs, modals—have strictly 0px border radius. This sharp geometry cuts through the rounded physical corners of modern smartphones, creating a striking contrast.

name: Bold Choices (Non-Negotiable)
guidance: 1. Massive Typographic Hero: The opening screen must feature a headline where a single word might span the full width.

name: 2. The "Hard" Line: A 4px black line (border-b-4) must separate the Hero from the content.
guidance: 3. Sticky Section Headers: As you scroll, the section title (e.g., "01. INFO") sticks to the top with a bottom border, stacking on top of the previous one.

name: 4. No Hamburger Icon: Use the word "MENU" in JetBrains Mono instead of an icon, if space permits.
guidance: 5. Inverted Gallery: Image galleries have a Black background with images at full opacity.

name: 6. Editorial Inputs: Form labels look like subheadings (Playfair Display, Italic).
guidance: 7. No Skeletons: Loading states are simple spinning black lines or just whitespace. No gray pulsing blobs.

name: 8. Mechanical Feedback: Every interactive element MUST have a visible active state (color inversion).

Design Philosophy
guidance: Core Principles: Precision, tactile depth, and fluid motion define this mobile design system. For React Native, every component is treated as a physical layer within a stacked 3D space, illuminated by virtual light sources. The design communicates "high-end mobile utility"—as responsive and refined as the Linear mobile app or Apple’s native interface. Every shadow uses multiple layers, every gradient is dithered to prevent banding, and every interaction uses precise haptic feedback combined with Expo.out easing.

name: Vibe: Cinematic Technical Minimalism. It’s a "Dark Mode First" aesthetic, utilizing near-blacks (#050506) and deep indigos. Imagine a high-end pro tool interface viewed through frosted glass at night. It is technical but inviting, using soft "glow" sources (#5E6AD2) to guide the thumb. It should feel like a premium native iOS/Android app, not a web-wrapper.
guidance: Differentiation: The signature is Atmospheric Depth. While most mobile apps are flat, this system uses:

name: ● Layered Backgrounds: Stacked View layers with varying opacities, blur intensities, and subtle noise textures.
guidance: ● Animated Ambient Light: Large, slow-moving blurred Canvas objects (using react-native-skia or Animated) that simulate light pools.

name: ● Haptic-Linked Interactions: Every "Pressable" event is paired with subtle haptics (Impact Light/Medium) to reinforce the "hardware" feel.
guidance: ● Spring-less Precision: Eschewing "bouncy" animations for sophisticated Bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) curves.

Core Principle
guidance: Clarity through structure, character through bold detail. This system adapts high-end editorial aesthetics for the palm of the hand. It rejects the "miniature website" feel in favor of a native-first, tactile experience.

name: Whitespace is a precision instrument for thumb-driven navigation. Motion is not decoration; it is spatial feedback. Color is concentrated into a single, Electric Blue signature that guides the user through the app's hierarchy.
"High-Tech Boutique." Imagine a premium fintech app combined with a modern design portfolio. It feels engineered, yet artistic; minimal, yet alive.

guidance: System Prompt: React Native Terminal CLI Framework

name: Design Philosophy
guidance: The Mobile Terminal CLI aesthetic adapts the raw power of a command-line interface into a handheld, touch-first environment. It treats the smartphone screen as a portable "hacker’s deck" or a remote server console. It is utilitarian, high-contrast, and uncompromisingly rigid.

name: The vibe is "Field Operative" or "System Administrator on the go." Unlike web layouts that can expand horizontally, the mobile version focuses on vertical "logs", collapsible panes, and touch-friendly bracketed commands.
● Monospace Supremacy: Every UI element uses monospaced fonts. Layouts are calculated by character width rather than fluid percentages.
guidance: ● The Command Line Prompt: Every screen starts with a system path or user breadcrumb (e.g., ~/root/user/home).

name: ● Tactile Feedback: Subtle haptics on every "keystroke" or button press to mimic mechanical keyboard feedback.
guidance: ● ASCII Borders: Using characters like +, -, |, and * to define UI boundaries instead of standard mobile shadows or rounded containers.

name: React Native implementations should use the StyleSheet or Tailwind/NativeWind tokens below:
guidance: ● Background: #050505 (Deepest black for OLED power saving and high contrast).

name: ○ Error: #FF3333 (Command Failed Red).
guidance: ● Border: #33FF00 (1px solid green for terminal windows).

name: Typography
guidance: ● Font Family: Courier New, SpaceMono-Regular (iOS/Android default), or bundled JetBrains Mono.

name: ● Size: Strict 12pt, 14pt, or 16pt. No "in-between" sizes.
guidance: ● Weight: Normal (monospaced fonts lose their character when too bold).

name: ● Line Height: Tight. 1.2x font size to maximize information density on small screens.
● Radius: 0. Standard React Native borderRadius: 0. No exceptions.
● Structure: Text wrapped in TouchableHighlight. Displayed as [ EXECUTE ] or > PROCEED.
guidance: ● Interaction: On press, underlayColor should be the Primary Green, and text color should flip to #000000(Inverted Video).

name: ● Structure: A <View> with borderWidth: 1 and borderColor: primary.
guidance: ● Title Bar: A top row with inverted colors: [ TITLE: SYSTEM_LOG ].

name: ● Scroll: Use FlatList with indicatorStyle="white" to mimic a scrolling terminal buffer.
● Style: No background. Starts with a prefix: $ or >.
guidance: ● The Cursor: A View with a blinking Opacity animation (0 to 1) placed at the end of the text string.

name: The layout mimics a mobile tmux session.
guidance: ● Vertical Stacking: Use Flexbox with flexDirection: 'column'. Screens should feel like a continuous stream of data.

name: ● Separators: Create a <Separator /> component that renders a string of dashes: --------------------------.
guidance: ● Status Bar: A permanent fixed footer showing [BATTERY: 88%] [NET: CONNECTED] [TIME: 08:51].

name: Non-Genericness (The Bold Factor)
guidance: ● Boot Sequence: On app launch, show a rapid-fire scroll of fake system logs (INIT_KERNEL..., LOADING_UI...) before the main content appears.

name: ● ASCII Graphics: Use Text components for headers instead of PNG icons.
● Progress Indicators: No ActivityIndicator. Use text-based bars: [#####-----] 50%.
● Blink: A 500ms loop of opacity for the cursor █.
guidance: ● Typewriter: Use a custom hook to slice strings from 0 to n characters over time for new data arrivals.

name: ● Scanlines: A top-level <ImageBackground> or absolute <View> with a repeating horizontal line pattern at 0.05opacity.
● Style: Use standard MaterialCommunityIcons or Lucide, but always set strokeWidth={1} and size={20}.
guidance: ● Implementation: Icons should be treated as "Characters." They must be the same color as the text they accompany.

name: Accessibility & Performance
guidance: ● Contrast: Green on Black is highly legible for vision-impaired users.

name: ● Touch Targets: Even though the style is "tight," ensure hitSlop is used on bracketed buttons to meet 44x44dp mobile standards.
guidance: ● Reduced Motion: Respect AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled by disabling the typewriter and scanline effects if requested.

name: Kinetic（动力学/动态）
guidance: 运动优先设计，排版是主要的视觉媒介。具有无限选框、视口缩放文本、滚动触发动画和激进的大写样式。高对比度的野兽派能量，有节奏的动作。

guidance: 2. 具有强引导需求的复杂流程 (Guidance)

name: 3. 数据可视化与实时监控 (Data Visualization)

Core Principle: Typography is the architecture of the mobile screen. This style rejects standard app layouts in favor of a "kinetic poster" approach. Text is treated as a graphic element; headers are hero units. Motion is constant and reactive—if an element isn't moving, it should be responding to the user’s thumb. Every interaction feels heavy, tactile, and high-contrast.
guidance: Aesthetic Vibe: High-energy street brutalism. Precision meets raw power. It is an underground zine optimized for a 6-inch screen. Everything is uppercase, oversized, and uncomfortably bold. It screams rather than whispers. Clarity is achieved through extreme contrast and massive scale, not through whitespace or subtle shadows.

name: Visual DNA: Relentless motion and aggressive scale. Numbers tower over labels. Scrolling isn't just movement—it's a performance. Use hard edges ($0px$ radius), sharp $2px$ borders, and instant color flips. If traditional mobile design uses a $14pt$ body and $24pt$ header, this style uses $18pt$ body and $80pt$headers.
● Infinite Marquees: Horizontal scrolling text that never stops (using react-native-reanimated).
guidance: ● Aggressive Typography: Display text is ALWAYS uppercase with tight tracking.

name: ● Massive Numerical Elements: Numbers ($60-120pt$) used as background graphic textures.
guidance: ● Tactile Color Inversions: On press, cards flood with accent color instantly (no slow fades).

name: ● Scroll-Driven Scaling: Elements scale or rotate based on scroll position (using Interpolate).
guidance: ● Brutalist Geometry: $2px$ solid borders, $0px$ border-radius, hairline grid dividers.

name: Typography System
guidance: ● Font Selection: Geometric Sans-Serifs (e.g., Space Grotesk, Inter, or System Bold).

name: ● Type Rules:
guidance: ○ Uppercase: All display headings, buttons, and navigation items.

name: ○ Letter Spacing: letterSpacing: -1 for large text, +2 for small labels.
guidance: ○ Line Height: lineHeight should be $0.9x$ to $1.1x$ the font size for headings to create a "blocky" look.

name: ● Shadows: NONE. Use color layering and borders for depth.
guidance: ● Padding: Aggressive. Section padding py: 40, Card padding p: 24.

name: ● Structure: Height 64px or 80px. Bold, uppercase text.
● Outline: borderWidth: 2, Border #3F3F46, Transparent background.
guidance: ● Interaction: Use Pressable with useAnimatedStyle. On onPressIn, scale to 0.95. On onPressOut, return to 1.0.

name: ● Interaction: On press, the background should "flood" with #DFE104 and text should flip to black.
guidance: ● Stacking: Use zIndex and translateY to create overlapping "sticky" card effects during scroll.

name: Inputs
guidance: ● Styling: Height 80px. borderBottomWidth: 2. Large uppercase text ($24pt+$).

name: ● Focus: Border color flips to Acid Yellow. Placeholder text in Muted Zinc.
● High Energy: Speed 5s per loop, no easing (easing: Linear).
guidance: ● Rule: No gradient fades at the edges. Text should clip sharply at the screen bounds.

name: Scroll-Triggered Transforms
guidance: ● Hero Parallax: As the user scrolls, the hero text should scale from 1.0 to 1.3 and fade to 0.

name: ● Sticky Header: Headers should snap and "push" the previous section's content.
● Haptic Feedback: Trigger Haptics.impactAsync(Haptics.ImpactFeedbackStyle.Medium) on every button press and card flip.
guidance: ● Instant Transitions: Color changes should be near-instant ($100ms$) to maintain the "brutalist" feel. Scale transforms can be slightly smoother ($300ms$).

name: Implementation Guidelines (React Native Specifics)
guidance: ● Performance: Use the Native Thread for all animations (Reanimated). Avoid setState for scroll animations.

name: ● Responsive Scaling: Use a helper function to scale font sizes based on screen width (windowWidth / 375 * size).
guidance: ● Safe Areas: Ensure massive headers don't collide with the notch/status bar using react-native-safe-area-context.

name: ● Accessibility:
guidance: ○ Maintain high contrast ratios ($15:1$ for text).

name: ○ Ensure accessibilityRole is defined for the massive custom buttons.
guidance: ○ Respect isReduceMotionEnabled to stop marquees for sensitive users.

guidance: 一种以去除深度线索（阴影、斜面、渐变）为中心的设计理念，有利于纯色、排版和布局。清晰、二维和几何，带有大胆的色块。

name: System Prompt: React Native Flat Design Specialist
guidance: Flat Design for mobile removes all artifice. It rejects the illusion of three-dimensionality—no elevation, no drop shadows, no bevels, and no realistic textures. It relies entirely on hierarchy through size, color, and typography. This is confident reduction tailored for the palm of the hand.

name: The aesthetic is "Mobile-First Poster Design": crisp edges, solid blocks of color, and high-impact layouts. Every element is a functional touch target. Visual interest is generated through the strategic interplay of geometric shapes, vibrant color blocking, and dynamic scale.
● Zero Elevation: The elevation (Android) and shadowColor (iOS) properties are strictly set to 0 or null. Hierarchy is created through color contrast and scale, never depth.
guidance: ● Color as Structure: Sections are defined by bold, full-width background colors rather than borders or shadows. Transitions are sharp and immediate.

name: ● Typography-First Interface: In the absence of shadows, font weight and size bear the weight of the UI. Use geometric, bold fonts that demand attention.
guidance: ● Geometric Purity: Perfect circles and squares. Rounded corners must be consistent (borderRadius). No "organic" or "hand-drawn" shapes.

name: ● Tactile Feedback: Since "hover" doesn't exist on mobile, use Pressable with immediate color shifts or scale-down effects (transform: [{ scale: 0.96 }]) to provide instant feedback.
● Accent: #F59E0B (Amber 500) - Warning/Attention.
guidance: ● Border: #E5E7EB (Gray 200) - Used only when color-blocking fails.

name: ● Border Radius: 6 (Small), 12 (Medium/Cards), 999 (Pill/Circular).
guidance: ● Touch Targets: Minimum height/width of 48 for all interactive elements.

name: Buttons (Pressable / TouchableOpacity)
guidance: ● Primary: backgroundColor: '#3B82F6', borderRadius: 8, height: 56, justifyContent: 'center'. White text.

name: ● Interaction: Use Pressable API: transform: [{ scale: pressed ? 0.97 : 1 }] and slight opacity or background darkening on press.
● Style: "Flat Block".
guidance: ● Appearance: backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF' (on Gray background) or solid tints (e.g., #DBEAFE for Blue-50).

name: ● Rules: shadowOpacity: 0, elevation: 0, padding: 20, borderRadius: 12.
guidance: ● Layout: Use flexDirection: 'column' or 'row' with hard alignments. No subtle gradients.

name: ● Focused: borderWidth: 2, borderColor: '#3B82F6', backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF'. No outer "glow."
● Flat Headers: Use a solid primary color background for the Header. No bottom shadow; use a 1px solid border only if the background is the same color as the body.
guidance: ● Bottom Tabs: Use active/inactive colors (#3B82F6 vs #9CA3AF). No "floating" tab bars; stick to the bottom with a solid color fill.

name: ● Color Blocking: Alternate screen sections using full-width View components with contrasting background colors (e.g., a Blue Hero View followed by a White Content View).
● Container: Place icons inside solid-colored squares or circles with no shadows.
● Avoid: Subtle drop shadows on buttons, "Soft UI" (Neumorphism), or standard iOS/Android system-default styling.
○ Scale: Make primary headlines much larger than body text for "Poster" impact.
guidance: ○ Decoration: Use position: 'absolute' views to place large, low-opacity geometric shapes (circles/rotated squares) behind content.

name: ○ Hard Borders: Use borderWidth: 4 for high-impact elements like Featured CTA buttons.
有趣、动态的颜色提取、药丸形状的按钮和不同的仰角状态。基于谷歌的Material Design 3，具有增强的深度和微观交互。

name: System Prompt: Material You (MD3) for React Native

Core Principles: Personal, adaptive, and spirited. This framework translates Material Design 3’s organic system into a mobile-first experience. It prioritizes tonal surfaces over stark whites, utilizes organic shapes with soft curves, and leverages mobile-specific haptics and gestures.
guidance: Vibe: Friendly, soft, rounded, and tactile. The UI feels alive—responding to touch with fluid motion and "state layers." Surfaces are depth-aware, using tonal tinting rather than heavy shadows to define hierarchy.

name: Mobile-Specific Implementation Details:
guidance: ● Touch-First Feedback: Every interactive element utilizes Pressable with a state layer and subtle scaletransformations.

name: ● Haptic Integration: Success, warning, and heavy interactions (like FAB presses) trigger HapticFeedback for a physical feel.
guidance: ● Atmospheric Depth: Since CSS backdrop-blur is heavy on mobile, we use layered View components with LinearGradient and absolute-positioned blurred shapes to simulate depth.

name: ● Safe Area Awareness: Full compliance with react-native-safe-area-context to ensure organic shapes flow behind status bars and home indicators.
guidance: ● Fluid Motion: Powered by Reanimated using the signature Material "Emphasized" easing.

name: Main CTAs, active tab icons, focus states
Chips, inactive toggle tracks, subtle buttons
State Layer Rules (Opacity Overlays):
guidance: ● Pressed State (Solid): Overlay black at 10% or white at 15% depending on background brightness.

name: ● Pressed State (Ghost): Primary color at 12% opacity.
guidance: ● Disabled: 38% opacity on both container and content.

name: Typography
guidance: Font Family: Roboto (System default on Android; load via Google Fonts for iOS parity).

name: ● Letter Spacing: Buttons and Labels use 0.1px tracking for readability at small sizes.
● Medium (16px): Standard Cards, Selection Dialogs.
guidance: ● Large (28px): Main Screen Cards, Bottom Sheets.

name: ● Full (Pills): All Buttons, Search Bars, FABs.
Shadows & Effects
guidance: Mobile elevation is primarily achieved through Tonal Elevation (overlaying a color) rather than just shadows.

name: ● Shadow Style: Use shadowColor with 0 offset and high blur for iOS; use elevation for Android.
○ Level 0: No shadow, flat surface.
guidance: ○ Level 1 (Cards): Subtle depth for list items.

name: ○ Level 2 (Active Cards): Enhanced depth for dragged or pressed items.
● Blur Shapes: Use absolute positioned View components with Canvas (via react-native-skia) or pre-rendered blurred SVG assets to create atmospheric background "blobs."
● Feedback: Must use Pressable with an Animated.View inside to handle scale: 0.95 on press.
guidance: ● FAB: 56x56dp, Tertiary color, borderRadius: 16 (rounded square) or 28 (circular).

name: Inputs (M3 Filled Style)
guidance: ● Visuals: Background #E7E0EC, flat bottom with 2px indicator.

name: ● Animation: Label floats upward on focus using Reanimated. Bottom border expands from center.
● Radius: 24px-28px.
guidance: ● Separation: Use Surface Container color against the Surface background. Avoid harsh borders.

name: Animation & Motion
guidance: All animations must use the Material Emphasized Easing:

name: 1. Haptic Choreography: Light haptics on toggle, medium on long-press.
guidance: 2. Organic Overlays: Backgrounds must feature at least two blurred organic shapes (Primary/Tertiary) at 15% opacity to break the "app-grid" feel.

name: 3. Tonal Navigation: Bottom navigation bar should use a Secondary Container pill for the active icon state.
guidance: 4. Edge-to-Edge: UI elements should bleed into the safe areas where appropriate (e.g., hero images) to feel modern.

name: Anti-Patterns (Avoid)
guidance: ● ❌ No Square Buttons: Everything interactive is pill-shaped or heavily rounded.

name: ● ❌ No Pure White: Use #FFFBFE for screens.
guidance: ● ❌ No Default Opacity Cuts: Use the Tonal Palette tokens for states, not just opacity: 0.5.

name: ● ❌ No Harsh Shadows: If it looks like a "drop shadow," it's too heavy. It should look like an "ambient glow."

name: Design Philosophy: Mobile Neo-Brutalism (React Native)
guidance: Neo-brutalism in a mobile context is a high-energy rebellion against the "Glassmorphism" and "Minimalist" trends of iOS and Android. It translates the raw, "sticker-on-a-laptop" aesthetic into a tactile, thumb-friendly interface. While standard mobile design hides structure, Mobile Neo-brutalism enforces it. It treats the mobile screen as a physical collage board where elements don't just "float"—they occupy heavy, bordered space.

name: Core DNA & Fundamental Principles:
guidance: ● Unapologetic Visibility: Reject subtle elevation. If a component exists, it must have a thick black border(borderWidth: 4). No soft shadows; use solid offset blocks for depth.

name: ● Tactile Feedback (The "Mechanical" Tap): Interactions shouldn't feel like "touching glass." Buttons must feel like physical switches. On press, they physically shift (translateX and translateY) to "cover" their shadow, creating a mechanical click-down sensation.
guidance: ● Sticker Layering: Treat screens as a series of layered paper cutouts. Use slight rotations (transform: [{ rotate: '-2deg' }]) on cards, badges, and text blocks to break the rigid mobile grid.

name: ● High-Voltage Palette: Use a "Pop Art" color scheme. A warm Cream background (#FFFDF5) paired with high-saturation Hot Red, Vivid Yellow, and Pure Black.
guidance: ● Anti-Smooth Motion: Avoid slow, easing transitions. Use snappy, spring-based animations or immediate state changes. Movement should feel like an arcade game, not a luxury car dashboard.

name: ● Background: #FFFDF5 (Cream) - The default canvas.
guidance: ● Ink/Stroke: #000000 (Pure Black) - Used for ALL text, borders, and shadows.

name: ● Muted: #C4B5FD (Soft Violet) - Tertiary/Card headers.
guidance: ● White: #FFFFFF - High contrast on dark backgrounds.

name: 2. Typography (Bold & Heavy)
guidance: ● Font Family: Space Grotesk (or System Bold if unavailable).

name: ● Border Radius: 0 (Sharp) is default. 999 (Pill) only for badges/special buttons.
guidance: ● Hard Shadows: Non-blurry, solid black offsets.

name: ● Structure: height: 56, borderWidth: 4, borderColor: '#000', borderRadius: 0.
guidance: ● Shadow: Apply a solid black View behind the button offset by 4px.

name: ● Interaction: Use Pressable. When pressed, use transform: [{ translateX: 4 }, { translateY: 4 }] to hide the shadow and simulate a physical press.
● Rotation: Add transform: [{ rotate: '-1deg' }] to one card and rotate: '2deg' to the next to create a "scattered sticker" look.
guidance: ● Padding: Aggressive padding (p: 20) to prevent text from touching the thick borders.

name: ● Focus State: Change backgroundColor to Yellow (#FFD93D) when focused. No soft "glow" or blue rings.
● Style: Circular or rectangular with borderWidth: 2.
guidance: ● Positioning: Use position: 'absolute' to "slap" them onto the corners of cards or images, often rotated.

name: Layout & Architecture
guidance: 1. The "Staggered" Grid: Avoid perfectly aligned columns. Use marginTop or marginLeft offsets to make elements feel hand-placed.

name: 2. Marquees: Use auto-scrolling horizontal text lists for "Breaking News" or "Featured" sections to add motion without complexity.
guidance: 3. Iconography: Use thick-stroke icons (e.g., Lucide-ReactNative with strokeWidth={3}). Always wrap icons in a bordered square or circle.

name: 4. Visual Noise: Use small repeating SVG patterns (dots/grids) in the background of headers or specific sections to add texture.
● No Linear Gradients: Use solid color blocks only.
guidance: ● No Shadow Radius: Blur radius must always be 0.

name: ● No Subtle Grays: Use #000 or the palette colors.
guidance: ● No Soft Easing: Animations should be Spring or Linear.

name: ● No "Standard" Border Radius: Avoid borderRadius: 8 or 12. It’s either 0 or 999.

name: Bold Typography Mobile is the translation of editorial poster design into a handheld experience. In this mobile-first environment, typography is the interface. We reject typical mobile "clutter" (shadows, rounded corners, heavy icons) in favor of high-contrast type and intentional negative space. The goal is to make a 6.7-inch screen feel like a premium printed broadsheet.
● Type as Hero: Headlines aren't just titles; they are the UI. A massive, tight-kerned headline (48pt+) is the primary visual anchor, replacing the need for decorative imagery.
guidance: ● Extreme Scale Contrast: Maintain a dramatic ratio between H1 and Body. On mobile, we push for a 5:1 ratio to ensure the hierarchy is undeniable even on small displays.

name: ● Deliberate Negative Space: Whitespace is used to frame "typographic blocks." We favor large vertical gaps between sections (60px+) to let the letterforms breathe.
guidance: ● Strict Hierarchy: The eye must follow a linear, editorial path: Massive Headline → Subhead (Mono) → Body → High-contrast CTA.

name: ● Restrained Palette: Near-black and warm-white. A single "Vermillion" accent for interaction. Color never decorates; it only indicates importance or action.
Confident. Editorial. High-end. It feels like a luxury brand's mobile app or a digital design manifesto. Every interaction is decisive, and every word is essential.
● Edge-to-Edge Typography: Headlines that push the horizontal bounds of the screen.
guidance: ● No Rounded Corners: borderRadius: 0 across all buttons, inputs, and containers.

name: ● Underlines as Primary UI: Interactive text is identified by thick (2pt-3pt) accent underlines.
guidance: ● Sharp Transitions: Instant or high-speed transitions. No "bouncing" or "elastic" animations.

name: ● Text: Accent color, fontWeight: '600', textTransform: 'uppercase', letterSpacing: 1.5.
guidance: ● Decoration: A View acting as a bottom border: height: 2, backgroundColor: accent, marginTop: 4.

name: ● No Shadows: Use borderBottomWidth: 1 and borderColor: border to separate content.
guidance: ● Sharp Edges: borderRadius: 0 for all view wrappers.

name: ● Section Spacing: Use paddingVertical: 64 as a standard for section breaks.
guidance: ● Accent Anchor: A small decorative View (width: 40, height: 4, backgroundColor: accent) placed above section titles.

name: Inputs
guidance: ● height: 56, backgroundColor: input, borderWidth: 1, borderColor: border, borderRadius: 0.

name: ● Staggered Layout: Use asymmetric margins (e.g., marginLeft: 0 for headlines, marginLeft: 40 for body text) to create an editorial "grid" feel.
guidance: ● Verticality: Prioritize a single-column flow with massive vertical gaps.

name: ● Bleed: Large decorative type (like section numbers "01") should partially bleed off the screen edge (right: -20).
● Easing: Easing.bezier(0.25, 0, 0, 1).
guidance: ● Entrance: Fade in + subtle slide up (10px) for text blocks.

name: ● Interactive: Pressing a card should result in a color shift (background from black to muted), not a "lift" or shadow effect.
● Size: 20px for UI controls, 32px for feature anchors.
guidance: ● Strict Rule: Icons must always be accompanied by a Mono-stack text label. Icons never stand alone unless they are standard navigation (e.g., Back arrow).

name: ● Touch Targets: All buttons/links must have a minimum hitSlop or height of 44px.
guidance: ● Readability: Body text never goes below 16px to ensure legibility on high-DPI mobile screens.

name: ● Visual Cues: Since we avoid shadows/depth, focus and active states must use the Accent Color (#FF3D00) or high-contrast inversion to indicate selection.

guidance: System Prompt: Scholarly Academia Mobile (React Native)

name: Design Philosophy
guidance: Core Principles: Scholarly gravitas meets timeless elegance, optimized for the handheld experience. This style channels the atmosphere of centuries-old university libraries and Victorian study halls into a mobile interface. Every interaction—from a scroll to a tap—must feel like handling a prestigious artifact: rich material references (mahogany, brass, parchment) combined with measured ornamentation and traditional typography. Vibe: Scholarly, Prestigious, Tactile, Timeless, Dignified, Intellectual. The Academia Mobile Promise: This is not a flat mobile app. It is a digital "pocket watch" or "leather-bound journal." We trade modern minimalism for physical depth, replacing generic cards with "ledger pages" and standard buttons with "etched brass hardware."

System Prompt: High-Fidelity Claymorphism (React Native Mobile Edition)

Core Concept: Digital Clay (Mobile) This design system is a high-fidelity simulation of a tangible, physical world constructed from premium digital clay. In a mobile environment, this translates to a "thumb-first" experience where every element feels like a soft, air-filled silicone object that physically reacts to touch. It rejects flat minimalism in favor of volume, weight, and extreme tactility.
guidance: The "High-Fidelity" Difference: Unlike standard mobile UI, this system uses multi-layered Shadow Stacks(simulated via nested View components or react-native-shadow-2) to create density. Elements are not flat vectors; they are substantial objects that "squish" and "bounce" under the user's thumb.

name: Visual Language & Materiality
guidance: ● Material: Soft-touch matte silicone and marshmallow-like foam. Surfaces absorb light; reflections are soft and diffused.

name: ● Lighting: A virtual overhead light source (top-left). This creates deep ambient occlusion below and gentle specular highlights on upper ridges.
○ Buttons/Inputs: borderRadius: 20

guidance: Here is a translated version of your web design prompt, adapted specifically for mobile application development (iOS/Android/React Native/Flutter). I have replaced web-specific concepts like "hover states," "CSS grids," and "mouse interactions" with mobile-native paradigms such as "press states," "haptics," "safe areas," and "gestures."

name: App Design System Prompt: Modern Enterprise SaaS (Mobile)

This style embodies the modern enterprise SaaS aesthetic translated for mobile — professional yet approachable, sophisticated yet tactile. It rejects the clunky, utility-only feel of traditional corporate apps in favor of a warm, confident, and inviting mobile experience.
● Trustworthy Yet Vibrant: Establishes credibility through clean structure and mobile-optimized typography, while maintaining visual energy through vibrant gradients.
guidance: ● Tactile Depth: Uses colored shadows, layered elevations (like bottom sheets and cards), and subtle z-axis stacking to create visual hierarchy on a small screen.

name: ● Refined Elegance: Every element is polished, focusing on fluid gestures, spring-physics animations, and sophisticated press states.
guidance: ● Purposeful Gradients: Indigo-to-violet gradients serve as the visual signature for primary CTAs and active states.

name: ● Mobile Polish: Generous spacing, safe-area awareness, and crisp typography create a premium, enterprise-ready feel in the palm of the hand.
guidance: Keywords: Trustworthy, Vibrant, Polished, Tactile, Modern, Approachable, Enterprise-Ready, Fluid.

name: ● Background: #F8FAFC (Slate 50) - A very subtle cool grey/white base for screen backgrounds.
guidance: ● Surface: #FFFFFF (White) - For cards, bottom sheets, and elevated elements.

name: Typography
guidance: ● Font Family: Plus Jakarta Sans — A geometric sans-serif that balances professional authority with modern approachability. High legibility on mobile screens.

name: ● Scaling: Designed to support iOS Dynamic Type and Android font scaling.
● Line Heights: Tight for titles (1.1-1.2), relaxed for body text (1.4-1.5) to ensure comfortable reading on narrow screens.
● Screen Elements: 16pt/dp for large cards and bottom sheets.
guidance: ● Interactive Elements: 8pt/dp for inputs and small chips.

name: ● Buttons: Fully rounded (pill shape) or 12pt/dp for a modern tech feel.
guidance: ● Borders: Hairline (0.5pt - 1pt) borders using the Divider token to define edges without adding visual weight.

name: Shadows & Depth
guidance: ● Card Elevation: Soft, colored shadows replacing neutral grays. Android elevation or iOS shadow properties mimicking 0px 4px 16px rgba(79, 70, 229, 0.08).

name: ● Bottom Sheet Shadow: Stronger upward shadow to indicate the highest z-index.
guidance: ● Button Shadow (Primary): 0px 4px 12px rgba(79, 70, 229, 0.25) to make primary actions highly prominent.

name: ● Primary CTA: Full width (minus screen padding) at the bottom of the screen. Background uses the Indigo-to-Violet gradient. White text.
guidance: ● Secondary: White surface, slate border, slate text.

name: ● Press State: Instead of web hover, use an active press state that slightly scales the button down (0.95x) and reduces shadow, paired with subtle haptic feedback.
● Base: White background, 16pt radius, hairline border, soft colored shadow.
guidance: ● Layout: Horizontal scrolling (carousels) for feature discovery, vertical standard lists for data.

name: ● List Items: Edge-to-edge with inset dividers, utilizing leading icons (in soft-colored circular backgrounds).
● Style: White background, 8pt radius, light slate border.
guidance: ● Active/Focused State: Border transitions to Indigo, label animates to a floating position, keyboard automatically presents.

name: ● Error State: Red border with an immediate, subtle shake animation.
● Haptic Integration: Tied to micro-interactions. Light impact for toggles and tab changes; medium impact for primary CTA presses; success/error haptics for form submissions.
guidance: ● Fluid Gestures: Bottom sheets that can be dragged and dismissed fluidly. Cards that support swipe-to-action (e.g., swipe left to archive) with color reveals underneath.

name: ● Scroll-Linked Animations: As the user scrolls down a list, the large screen title collapses smoothly into the top navigation bar.
guidance: ● Gradient Usage: Used sparingly but intentionally—on the active tab icon, primary buttons, and as a subtle blurred mesh background behind onboarding screens.

name: ● Skeletal Loading: Instead of traditional spinners, use animated, pulsing placeholder shapes (Indigo/Slate tinted) that match the layout of the incoming data.
● Safe Areas: Strict adherence to iOS and Android safe area insets (notches, dynamic islands, home indicator areas).
guidance: ● Screen Padding: Consistent 16pt/dp or 20pt/dp horizontal margins for all screens.

name: ● Vertical Rhythm: 24pt/dp between distinct sections; 12pt/dp between related items in a group.
○ Bottom Tab Navigation for primary destinations (3-5 items).
guidance: ○ Top App Bar for contextual actions, screen titles, and back buttons.

name: ● Content Width: Text blocks should rarely span the absolute full width; use generous margins to keep line lengths comfortable for reading.
● Philosophy: "Fluid and Springy" — Animations should feel physical and interruptible.
guidance: ● Screen Transitions: Standard platform pushes (slide left on iOS, fade/scale on Android), but utilize Shared Element Transitions for hero images or core cards opening into detail views.

name: ● Press Animations: Scale-down (scale: 0.97) on press down, spring back on release.
guidance: ● Easing: Avoid linear animations. Use spring physics for layout changes and ease-out for opacities.

name: ● Style: 2px stroke width, rounded caps and joins for a friendly, modern feel.
guidance: ● Touch Targets: Crucial for mobile. Minimum tap area of 44x44 pt (iOS) or 48x48 dp (Android) for all interactive elements, even if the visual icon is only 24x24.

name: ● Active States: Navigation icons transition from outlined (inactive) to filled/gradient (active).
● Dynamic Text: UI must not break when users increase their system font size. Text must wrap or truncate gracefully.
guidance: ● Contrast: All text meets WCAG AA standards. Slate 900 on Slate 50 background is highly compliant.

name: ● Screen Readers: Ensure all image assets and icon buttons have descriptive accessibilityLabel (iOS) and contentDescription (Android).
guidance: ● Dark Mode Readiness: While this spec is light-mode first, tokens should be structured to easily invert (e.g., Slate 50 becomes Slate 900, gradients become slightly muted).

name: ● Reduced Motion: Respect OS-level "Reduce Motion" settings by falling back to simple cross-fades instead of complex scaling and spring animations.

name: 4. 游戏化 UI 与 叙事解谜游戏

The Hand-Drawn mobile design style celebrates authentic imperfection and human touch within the palm of the hand. It rejects the sterile precision of typical mobile UI in favor of organic, playful irregularity that evokes a portable sketchbook, a pocket notebook, or a collection of sticky notes.
● No Straight Lines: Every View and TouchableOpacity uses complex borderRadius logic or SVG masks to create wobbly edges that reject geometric perfection.
guidance: ● Tactile Texture: Use background overlays or tiled images to simulate physical media like grain, paper, or notebook grids, making the screen feel like a physical surface.

name: ● Playful Tilt: Elements utilize small transform: [{ rotate: '-1deg' }] properties to break the rigid mobile grid and create casual energy.
guidance: ● Hard Offset Shadows: Strictly avoid elevation (Android) or shadowRadius (iOS) that creates blur. Use a secondary "shadow layer" View offset by (4px, 4px) to create a cut-paper, layered collage aesthetic.

name: ● Handwritten Typography: Exclusively use handwritten fonts (Kalam, Patrick Hand). Typography should vary in size and rotation to look like quick notes written on the fly.
guidance: ● Scribbled Overlays: Use absolute-positioned SVGs for flourishes like hand-drawn arrows, "tape" effects on images, and "X" marks for close buttons.

name: ● Intentional Messiness: Embrace slight overlaps and asymmetrical margins that make the app feel spontaneous rather than manufactured.
guidance: ● Emotional Intent: This style lowers the "fear of interaction" by appearing unfinished and approachable. It is perfect for journaling apps, creative tools, education, or any product that wants to feel human-centered rather than corporate.

name: Typography
guidance: ● Headings: Kalam-Bold - High visual weight, felt-tip marker style.

name: ● Body: PatrickHand-Regular - Highly legible but distinctly human.
guidance: ● Scale: Large and readable for mobile. Headings should have lineHeight adjusted to allow for "scribbled" descenders.

name: Radius & Border
guidance: ● Wobbly Borders: Since React Native doesn't support the slash / syntax for borderRadius, use unique values for each corner (e.g., borderTopLeftRadius: 15, borderTopRightRadius: 25, borderBottomLeftRadius: 20, borderBottomRightRadius: 10).

name: ● Border Width: Bold and visible. borderWidth: 2 minimum; borderWidth: 3 for primary actions.
guidance: ● Style: solid for containers; dashed for "cut-out" sections or empty states.

name: Shadows & Effects
guidance: ● Hard Offset Shadows: Since shadowOpacity with 0 radius is inconsistent across platforms, implement as a "Shadow View" placed behind the main component.

name: ○ Standard: Offset { x: 4, y: 4 } with #2d2d2d.
guidance: ○ Active (Press): Offset { x: 0, y: 0 } (the button "sinks" into the paper).

name: ● Paper Texture: Use a repeating pattern background image or a very subtle radial-gradient SVG overlay across the entire screen.
● Normal State: * White background, borderWidth: 3, Pencil Black text.
guidance: ○ Hard offset shadow view behind it.

name: ○ The background "shadow view" is covered, simulating a physical press.
Cards/Containers
guidance: ● Base Style: White background, wobbly border, slight rotation (-1deg or 1deg).

name: ● Decoration Types:
guidance: ○ "Tape": A semi-transparent View (rgba(200, 200, 200, 0.5)) positioned absolutely at the top, slightly rotated to look like it holds the card.

name: ○ "Tack": A small red circle SVG at the top center.
guidance: ● Speech Bubbles: Use a small SVG triangle attached to the bottom of the container for "hint" text or tooltips.

name: Inputs (TextInput)
guidance: ● Style: Full box with wobbly borderRadius.

name: ● Font: PatrickHand-Regular.
guidance: ● Focus State: Border color changes to Blue Ballpoint (#2d5da1) with a slight increase in borderWidth.

name: ● The "Anti-Grid": Avoid perfect alignment. Give adjacent cards slightly different rotation values.
guidance: ● Layering: Use zIndex to stack "tape" over cards and cards over background scribbles.

name: ● Visual Rhythm: Use padding: 20 for standard screens. Use large gap values between vertical elements to let the "paper" breathe.
guidance: ● Overflow: Allow decorative elements (like a hand-drawn star) to bleed slightly off the edge of the screen.

name: Non-Genericness (Bold Choices)
guidance: ● Sketchy Loaders: Instead of a spinner, use an animation that "scribbles" a circle or toggles between three hand-drawn frames.

name: ● Hand-Drawn Icons: Use icons that look like they were drawn with a 0.5mm fineliner (thick strokes, unclosed loops).
guidance: ● Strikethrough: When a task is completed or an item is deleted, use a red "scribble-out" SVG overlay instead of a simple line.

name: ● Wavy Dividers: Replace standard horizontal rules with a hand-drawn squiggly line SVG.
● The "Jiggle": When a user triggers an error, use a Sequence animation that rapidly rotates the element between -2deg and 2deg.
guidance: ● Snappy Transitions: Use LayoutAnimation.configureNext(LayoutAnimation.Presets.spring) for all layout changes to give a bouncy, physical feel.

name: Mobile-First Strategy (React Native)
guidance: ● Touch Targets: Ensure all wobbly buttons have a minimum hit area of 48x48 even if the visual "ink" is smaller.

name: ● Safe Area: Ensure the "Paper" background extends into the SafeAreaView to maintain the illusion of physical media.
guidance: ● Performance: Use useNativeDriver: true for rotations and transforms to keep the "hand-drawn" movements fluid at 60fps.

name: ● Haptics: Add Haptics.impactAsync on button presses to reinforce the feeling of "clicking" a physical object.

Core Principles: Neumorphism on mobile creates a tactile, physical interface through dual shadows. On React Native, this is achieved by layering View components or using specialized libraries to simulate the light source (top-left) and dark shadow (bottom-right). Every component is "molded" from the base material (#E0E5EC). Buttons are never flat; they are either "pillowed" (convex) or "wells" (concave/inset).
guidance: Vibe: Premium, ceramic-like feel. It prioritizes the "thump" of a physical button press. It is a calm, monochromatic experience that uses depth rather than color to establish hierarchy.

name: Unique Visual Signatures (Mobile Specific):
guidance: ● Dual-Shadow Layering: Since React Native's shadowColor only supports one direction, use nested Viewcomponents or react-native-shadow-2 to achieve the dual-light effect.

name: ● Haptic Feedback: Every neumorphic interaction (Press/Release) must be accompanied by light haptic feedback (ImpactFeedbackStyle.Light).
guidance: ● Physical Press: Active states should use transform: [{ scale: 0.97 }] to simulate material compression.

name: ● Full-Screen Surface: The entire app uses a single background color (#E0E5EC) to maintain the "continuous material" illusion.
● Accent: #6C63FF (Used for active toggles or primary CTAs).
● Font Family: Use System (San Francisco/Roboto) or PlusJakartaSans-Bold.
● Letter Spacing: -0.5 for headings to enhance the modern look.
Note: React Native requires specific shadow properties for iOS and elevation for Android. For high-fidelity Neumorphism, use a wrapping implementation.
● Outer Shadow (Dark): shadowOffset: { width: 6, height: 6 }, shadowOpacity: 1, shadowRadius: 10, shadowColor: 'rgba(163, 177, 198, 0.7)'
guidance: ● Inner Shadow (Light): shadowOffset: { width: -6, height: -6 }, shadowOpacity: 1, shadowRadius: 10, shadowColor: 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.6)'

name: Inset (Concave - Pressed/Input)
guidance: ● Concept: Use a slightly darker background color (#D1D9E6) or internal gradients/shadows to simulate a "carved out" look.

name: ● Visual: backgroundColor: '#E0E5EC', plus internal borders of 1px with a darker shade at the top.
● Default State: Extruded dual shadow.
guidance: ● Active State (Pressable): Remove outer shadows, apply scale: 0.98, and change background slightly to indicate an "inset" state.

name: ● Base: padding: 24, borderRadius: 32, backgroundColor: '#E0E5EC'.
guidance: ● Hierarchy: Use "Nested Depth." A card (Extruded) contains an Icon Container (Inset Deep).

name: ● Depth: Must be Inset (Concave) to look like a physical slot.
guidance: ● Focus: Border color changes to #6C63FF with a thin 1.5px width.

name: Animation & Micro-interactions
guidance: ● Framework: Use React Native Reanimated or LayoutAnimation.

name: ● Transitions: Duration: 250ms, Easing: Bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1).
guidance: ● Interaction: * Scale: Buttons shrink by 2-3% when pressed.

name: ○ Shadow Transition: Smoothly interpolate shadow opacity from 1 to 0.4 when an element is "pressed" into the surface.
● Safe Area: Always wrap in SafeAreaView.
guidance: ● Touch Targets: Minimum 48x48 for all interactive elements.

name: ● Spacing: Use a 8pt grid system (8, 16, 24, 32, 48).
guidance: ● Contrast: Ensure text color maintains WCAG AA compliance against the #E0E5EC background.

name: Anti-Patterns (Do Not Do)
guidance: ● No Black Shadows: Never use rgba(0,0,0,x). Shadows must be blue-grey to match the "Cool Grey" material.

name: ● No Pure White Backgrounds: The screen background MUST be #E0E5EC.
● No Flat Borders: Do not use borderWidth: 1 as a substitute for depth unless in a focus state.
